reflection:
Pain woke you at 5:50 and the first thing you did was lie there for an hour with Joe Dispenza. That's not nothing.

The coccyx flare is probably connected to skipping amitriptyline the night before. Your body noticed the gap. And yet anxiety was at 2 and mood at 7, which means the nature setting is actually doing something real. Jezersko has you in a good place, whatever the pain says in the morning.

The worry from Tuesday, whether you can still get hard, whether to stop everything cold turkey - that's heavy and you're right to take it seriously. Reducing to 2x per week makes sense. But cold turkey is a different story. What you felt at 5:50 was a small demo of what your nervous system does when support drops fast.

From the CBT the day before: 8 down to 6. Your own reframe, "call it sick, they'll find a way", is worth holding onto. Delegation isn't failure.

One thing for today: don't make any permanent pill decisions until you're back home. Let the mountains do their work first.
opener: Pain spiked after skipping amitriptyline but anxiety stayed at 2 in Jezersko
focus_for_today: No permanent pill decisions until you're home - let the mountains work first
